Perfluorinated derivatives are fully fluorinated organic compounds, often used as refrigerants, aerosol propellants, or fire extinguishing agents. These include compounds such as perfluoromethane, perfluoroethane, and perfluoropropane. They are chemically inert, non-flammable, and have high boiling points. Their applications span industrial cooling, medical equipment, and electronics manufacturing due to their thermal and chemical stability.

Other chemical substances not specified elsewhere in this chapter include miscellaneous fluorinated or halogenated compounds. These may include industrial solvents, intermediates for pharmaceuticals, or specialty chemicals. They are typically used in polymer synthesis, metal treatment, or as processing aids. Specific applications vary based on molecular structure and purity.

These are hydrofluoroolefins (HFOs) — specifically 2,3,3,3-tetrafluoropropene (HFO-1234yf), 1,3,3,3-tetrafluoropropene (HFO-1234ze), and (Z)-1,1,1,4,4,4-hexafluoro-2-butene (HFO-1336mzz). Used as refrigerants and propellants, they are designed to replace ozone-depleting substances. These compounds are low-GWP alternatives, often used in automotive air conditioning systems and aerosol applications.

Other chemical substances not specified elsewhere in this chapter include miscellaneous halogenated or fluorinated compounds. These may be intermediates, solvents, or specialty chemicals used in manufacturing, pharmaceuticals, or industrial processes. Their applications depend on molecular composition and physical properties such as volatility, polarity, or reactivity.

Methyl bromide (bromomethane) is a colorless, volatile liquid used historically as a fumigant for soil, grain, and wood. It is also used as a chemical intermediate in the synthesis of other compounds. Due to its ozone-depleting potential, its use is restricted under international agreements. It is highly toxic and flammable under certain conditions.

Ethylene dibromide (1,2-dibromoethane) is a colorless, volatile liquid used as a fumigant and solvent. It is also employed in the synthesis of vinyl bromide and other brominated compounds. It is toxic, carcinogenic, and environmentally persistent. Its use is restricted in many applications due to health and environmental concerns, including its potential to contaminate groundwater.

Dibromomethane is a colorless, volatile liquid with a mild odor. It is used as a fumigant for stored products, a solvent in industrial processes, and a chemical intermediate. It is toxic and flammable, and its use is restricted in many countries due to health and environmental risks. It decomposes to release toxic fumes under certain conditions.

Other chemical substances not specified elsewhere in this chapter include miscellaneous halogenated or fluorinated compounds. These may be intermediates, solvents, or specialty chemicals used in manufacturing, pharmaceuticals, or industrial processes. Their applications depend on molecular composition and physical properties such as volatility, polarity, or reactivity.

Iodides are inorganic or organic compounds containing iodine bonded to other elements, such as alkali metals or hydrogen. These are used as catalysts, pharmaceuticals, or in the synthesis of organic iodides. They are often highly reactive, soluble in polar solvents, and may be corrosive or toxic depending on the specific compound and concentration.

Chlorodifluoromethane (HCFC-22) is a colorless, non-toxic gas used as a refrigerant and in the production of other fluorinated compounds. It is less ozone-depleting than CFCs but still contributes to stratospheric ozone depletion. It is also used as a chemical intermediate and in aerosol propellants. Its use is regulated under international agreements due to environmental concerns.

Dichlorotrifluoroethanes (HCFC-123) are colorless, volatile liquids used as refrigerants, solvents, and chemical intermediates. They are less ozone-depleting than CFCs but still contribute to stratospheric ozone depletion. They are used in industrial cleaning, refrigeration, and as feedstocks for fluorinated compounds. Their use is regulated under international agreements.

Dichlorofluoroethanes (HCFC-141, 141b) are colorless, volatile liquids used as refrigerants, solvents, and chemical intermediates. They are less ozone-depleting than CFCs but still contribute to stratospheric ozone depletion. They are used in industrial cleaning, refrigeration, and as feedstocks for fluorinated compounds. Their use is regulated under international agreements.

Chlorodifluoroethanes (HCFC-142, 142b) are colorless, volatile liquids used as refrigerants, solvents, and chemical intermediates. They are less ozone-depleting than CFCs but still contribute to stratospheric ozone depletion. They are used in industrial cleaning, refrigeration, and as feedstocks for fluorinated compounds. Their use is regulated under international agreements.

Dichloropentafluoropropanes (HCFC-225, 225ca, 225cb) are colorless, volatile liquids used as refrigerants and chemical intermediates. They are less ozone-depleting than CFCs but still contribute to stratospheric ozone depletion. They are used in industrial cleaning, refrigeration, and as feedstocks for fluorinated compounds. Their use is regulated under international agreements.

Bromochlorodifluoromethane (Halon-1211) is a colorless, non-toxic liquid used primarily as a fire suppression agent, especially for electrical and electronic equipment. It is highly effective against fires involving flammable liquids and gases. It is non-flammable and has low toxicity, but its use is restricted under international agreements due to its potential to deplete the ozone layer.

Bromotrifluoromethane (Halon-1301) is a colorless, non-toxic liquid used primarily as a fire suppression agent, especially for electrical and electronic equipment. It is highly effective against fires involving flammable liquids and gases. It is non-flammable and has low toxicity, but its use is restricted under international agreements due to its potential to deplete the ozone layer.

Dibromotetrafluoroethanes (Halon-2402) are colorless, non-toxic liquids used primarily as fire suppression agents, especially for electrical and electronic equipment. They are highly effective against fires involving flammable liquids and gases. They are non-flammable and have low toxicity, but their use is restricted under international agreements due to their potential to deplete the ozone layer.

These compounds include trichlorofluoromethane, dichlorodifluoromethane, trichlorotrifluoroethanes, dichlorotetrafluoroethanes, and chloropentafluoroethane. They are halogenated hydrocarbons used primarily as refrigerants, propellants, and chemical intermediates. Many are ozone-depleting and are regulated under international agreements. They are typically colorless and volatile, with varying degrees of toxicity and environmental persistence.

Other chemical substances not specified elsewhere in this chapter include miscellaneous halogenated or fluorinated compounds. These may be intermediates, solvents, or specialty chemicals used in manufacturing, pharmaceuticals, or industrial processes. Their applications depend on molecular composition and physical properties such as volatility, polarity, or reactivity.

Other perhalogenated derivatives are fluorinated or brominated compounds not specified elsewhere. These may include perfluorinated alcohols, ethers, or acids, used as surfactants, solvents, or chemical intermediates. They are typically chemically stable, non-flammable, and have high boiling points. Their applications vary based on molecular structure and purity.

These are halogenated compounds containing only bromine and chlorine, or fluorine and chlorine, or fluorine and bromine. They include various alkyl and aryl halides, used as solvents, refrigerants, or chemical intermediates. Many are toxic or environmentally persistent. Their applications are restricted under international agreements due to potential ozone depletion or toxicity.

Other chemical substances not specified elsewhere in this chapter include miscellaneous halogenated or fluorinated compounds. These may be intermediates, solvents, or specialty chemicals used in manufacturing, pharmaceuticals, or industrial processes. Their applications depend on molecular composition and physical properties such as volatility, polarity, or reactivity.

1,2,3,4,5,6-Hexachlorocyclohexane (HCH), including lindane, is a colorless, crystalline solid used historically as an insecticide. It is highly toxic and persistent in the environment. Lindane is a specific isomer used in agricultural and medical applications. Its use is restricted due to environmental and health concerns, including neurotoxicity and bioaccumulation.

Aldrin, chlordane, and heptachlor are organochlorine insecticides used historically to control pests in agriculture and public health. They are highly toxic, persistent, and bioaccumulative. Aldrin and chlordane are particularly toxic to birds and mammals. Their use is now restricted or banned in many countries due to environmental and health risks.

Mirex is a colorless, crystalline solid used historically as an insecticide, particularly for controlling termites and other pests. It is highly toxic, persistent, and bioaccumulative. Its use is now restricted or banned in many countries due to environmental and health risks, including potential carcinogenicity and toxicity to wildlife.

1,2-Dibromo-4-(1,2-dibromoethyl)cyclohexane and tetrabromocyclooctanes are halogenated cyclic compounds used historically as insecticides or flame retardants. They are highly toxic and persistent in the environment. Their use is now restricted or banned in many countries due to environmental and health risks, including neurotoxicity and bioaccumulation.

Other chemical substances not specified elsewhere in this chapter include miscellaneous halogenated or fluorinated compounds. These may be intermediates, solvents, or specialty chemicals used in manufacturing, pharmaceuticals, or industrial processes. Their applications depend on molecular composition and physical properties such as volatility, polarity, or reactivity.

Chlorobenzene, o-dichlorobenzene, and p-dichlorobenzene are aromatic compounds used as solvents, chemical intermediates, or in the production of dyes and pharmaceuticals. They are toxic, flammable, and potentially carcinogenic. Their use is regulated under environmental and occupational safety standards due to health and environmental risks.

Hexachlorobenzene and DDT are organochlorine compounds used historically as insecticides. Hexachlorobenzene is toxic and persistent in the environment. DDT is highly effective against pests but is toxic to birds and mammals and persists in ecosystems. Their use is now restricted or banned in many countries due to environmental and health risks.

Pentachlorobenzene is a colorless, crystalline solid used as a chemical intermediate or in the production of dyes and pharmaceuticals. It is toxic, persistent, and potentially carcinogenic. Its use is restricted under environmental and occupational safety standards due to health and environmental risks.

Hexabromobiphenyls are brominated aromatic compounds used as flame retardants in plastics, textiles, and electronics. They are persistent, bioaccumulative, and toxic. Their use is restricted or banned in many countries due to environmental and health risks, including neurotoxicity and endocrine disruption.

2,3,4,5,6-Pentabromoethylbenzene is a colorless, crystalline solid used as a chemical intermediate or in the production of flame retardants. It is toxic and persistent. Its use is restricted under environmental and occupational safety standards due to health and environmental risks.

Other chemical substances not specified elsewhere in this chapter include miscellaneous halogenated or fluorinated compounds. These may be intermediates, solvents, or specialty chemicals used in manufacturing, pharmaceuticals, or industrial processes. Their applications depend on molecular composition and physical properties such as volatility, polarity, or reactivity.

Derivatives containing only sulpho groups, their salts, and ethyl esters are used as surfactants, detergents, or chemical intermediates. These compounds are typically water-soluble and used in cleaning agents, paints, and pharmaceuticals. They are often non-toxic and biodegradable, though some may have environmental persistence.

Derivatives containing only nitro or nitroso groups are used as chemical intermediates or in the production of dyes, explosives, or pharmaceuticals. These compounds are typically reactive and may be toxic or environmentally persistent. Their applications are regulated under safety and environmental standards.

Perfluorooctane sulphonic acid is a fluorinated organic acid used as a surfactant, detergent, or chemical intermediate. It is highly stable, non-flammable, and has high thermal resistance. It is used in industrial cleaning, coatings, and pharmaceuticals. However, it is persistent and toxic, and its use is restricted under environmental regulations.

Ammonium perfluorooctane sulphonate is an ionic surfactant derived from perfluorooctane sulphonic acid. It is used in detergents, paints, and industrial cleaning agents. It is non-flammable and chemically stable. However, it is persistent and toxic, and its use is restricted under environmental regulations.

Lithium perfluorooctane sulphonate is an ionic surfactant derived from perfluorooctane sulphonic acid. It is used in detergents, paints, and industrial cleaning agents. It is non-flammable and chemically stable. However, it is persistent and toxic, and its use is restricted under environmental regulations.

Potassium perfluorooctane sulphonate is an ionic surfactant derived from perfluorooctane sulphonic acid. It is used in detergents, paints, and industrial cleaning agents. It is non-flammable and chemically stable. However, it is persistent and toxic, and its use is restricted under environmental regulations.

Salts of perfluorooctane sulphonic acid, excluding specified compounds, used in industrial and chemical applications, typically as surfactants or in specialty coatings, often appearing as crystalline or powdered solids.
Perfluorooctane sulphonyl fluoride, a fluorinated organic compound, used as a precursor in chemical synthesis, typically a colorless liquid or solid with high thermal stability.
Trichloronitromethane, also known as chloropicrin, a volatile, toxic compound used as a fumigant for pest control in agriculture and storage facilities, appearing as a colorless liquid or gas.

Long-chain alcohols including dodecan-1-ol (lauryl alcohol), hexadecan-1-ol (cetyl alcohol), and octadecan-1-ol (stearyl alcohol), used in surfactants, cosmetics, and lubricants, typically appearing as waxy solids or viscous liquids.

Miscellaneous chemical alcohols not classified elsewhere in Chapter 29, including undefined or unlisted compounds, typically used as solvents or intermediates.
Ethylene glycol (ethanediol), a diol used primarily as a coolant and solvent, typically appearing as a colorless, viscous liquid with a sweet odor.
Propylene glycol (propane-1,2-diol), a diol used as a solvent, humectant, and in food and pharmaceutical applications, typically appearing as a colorless, viscous liquid with a mild odor.
Butane-1,3-diol, a diol used as a solvent and in polymer synthesis, typically appearing as a colorless, viscous liquid with a mild odor.
Butane-1,4-diol or tetramethylene glycol (1,4-butanediol) with 100% bio-based carbon content, derived from renewable sources, used as a solvent and in polymer synthesis.

Mannitol, a sugar alcohol used as a sweetener, pharmaceutical excipient, and in food applications, typically appearing as a white crystalline powder.
D-glucitol (sorbitol) containing 2% or less by weight of D-mannitol, used as a sweetener and in pharmaceuticals, typically appearing as a white crystalline powder.

D-glucitol (sorbitol) containing 2% or less by weight of D-mannitol, used as a sweetener and in pharmaceuticals, typically appearing as a white crystalline powder.

Glycerol, a triol used as a humectant, solvent, and in food, pharmaceutical, and cosmetic applications, typically appearing as a colorless, viscous liquid.

Hydroquinone (quinol) and its salts, used as a reducing agent, in photographic developers, and as a skin-lightening agent in cosmetics and pharmaceuticals.

Methacrylic acid and its salts, including sodium methacrylate. These are used in the production of acrylic resins, adhesives, and coatings. They are typically colorless liquids or white crystalline solids with acidic properties.

Esters of methacrylic acid, including methyl methacrylate and ethyl methacrylate. These are used as monomers in polymer synthesis and as solvents in coatings and adhesives. They are typically colorless liquids with low viscosity.

Oleic, linoleic, and linolenic acids and their salts and esters, unsaturated fatty acids found in vegetable oils. They are used in lubricants, cosmetics, and as plasticizers or stabilizers in polymers. They are typically liquids at room temperature.
Binapacryl (ISO), an organic compound used as a chemical intermediate. It is typically a solid or liquid and may be used in the synthesis of polymers or as a solvent in industrial applications.

Undecenoic acid and its salts and esters, a medium-chain unsaturated fatty acid. It is used in the production of fragrances, lubricants, and as a chemical intermediate in pharmaceuticals or polymers. It is typically a liquid or crystalline solid.

Crotonic acid, a short-chain unsaturated carboxylic acid. It is used as a chemical intermediate in the synthesis of pharmaceuticals, dyes, and perfumes. It is typically a colorless liquid with a pungent odor.

Other chemical substances not classified elsewhere in Chapter 29, including esters, acids, or salts not covered by specific HS codes. These may include industrial-grade intermediates or specialty chemicals used in synthesis or processing.

Cyclanic, cyclenic, or cycloterpenic monocarboxylic acids and their derivatives, including anhydrides, halides, peroxides, and peroxyacids. These are used as intermediates in the synthesis of pharmaceuticals, polymers, and agrochemicals. They are typically liquids or solids.

Benzoic acid and its salts and esters, weak organic acids used as preservatives in food, cosmetics, and pharmaceuticals. They are typically white crystalline solids with a characteristic odor and are effective against mold and bacteria.

Benzoyl peroxide and benzoyl chloride, organic compounds used as initiators in polymerization reactions and as bleaching agents. Benzoyl peroxide is also used in dermatological treatments. They are typically solids or liquids with reactive properties.

Phenylacetic acid and its salts, organic acids used as intermediates in the synthesis of pharmaceuticals and fragrances. They are typically colorless liquids or white crystalline solids with acidic properties.

Esters of phenylacetic acid, including phenylacetate esters. These are used as intermediates in pharmaceuticals, fragrances, and as solvents in industrial applications. They are typically liquids or solids with characteristic odors.

Other chemical substances not classified elsewhere in Chapter 29, including esters, acids, or salts not covered by specific HS codes. These may include specialty chemicals used in industrial or laboratory applications.

Oxalic acid and its salts and esters, weak diprotic organic acids used as reducing agents, bleaching agents, and in metal cleaning. They are typically colorless crystals or liquids and are corrosive to metals and skin.

Adipic acid and its salts and esters, a dicarboxylic acid used as a monomer in the production of nylon-6,6. It is typically a white crystalline solid and is also used in the synthesis of pharmaceuticals and polymers.
Sebacic acid, a long-chain dicarboxylic acid used as a monomer in the production of polyamides and lubricants. It is typically a white crystalline solid and is also used as a plasticizer in polymers.

Other chemical substances not classified elsewhere in Chapter 29, including esters, acids, or salts not covered by specific HS codes. These may include industrial-grade intermediates or specialty chemicals used in synthesis or processing.

Maleic anhydride, a cyclic unsaturated dicarboxylic acid anhydride. It is used as a monomer in the production of polymers, resins, and as a cross-linking agent in coatings and adhesives. It is typically a white crystalline solid.

Malonic acid and its salts and esters, organic dicarboxylic acids used as chemical intermediates in the synthesis of pharmaceuticals and polymers. They are typically colorless crystalline solids with acidic properties.

Ethane-1,2-dicarboxylic acid (succinic acid) or butanedioic acid (succinic acid) with 100% bio-based carbon content. These are renewable, biodegradable acids used in the production of bioplastics, pharmaceuticals, and as chemical intermediates.

Other chemical substances not classified elsewhere in Chapter 29, including esters, acids, or salts not covered by specific HS codes. These may include industrial-grade intermediates or specialty chemicals used in synthesis or processing.

Cyclanic, cyclenic, or cycloterpenic polycarboxylic acids and their derivatives, including anhydrides, halides, peroxides, and peroxyacids. These are used as intermediates in the synthesis of pharmaceuticals, polymers, and agrochemicals. They are typically liquids or solids.

Dioctyl orthophthalate, an ester of orthophthalic acid used as a plasticizer in PVC and other polymers. It is typically a colorless liquid and is used to improve flexibility and durability in plastic products.

Dinonyl or didecyl orthophthalates, esters of orthophthalic acid used as plasticizers in polyvinyl chloride and other polymers. They are typically colorless liquids or solids and are used to enhance flexibility and processing properties.

Other esters of orthophthalic acid, including esters of phthalic acid used as plasticizers or additives in polymers. These are typically colorless liquids or solids and are used to improve processing and performance in plastic products.

Phthalic anhydride, a cyclic dicarboxylic acid anhydride used as a monomer in the production of phthalate esters and polyesters. It is typically a white crystalline solid and is used in the manufacture of plastics, resins, and coatings.

Terephthalic acid and its salts are crystalline organic compounds used primarily as monomers in polyester production. Common salts include sodium and calcium terephthalates. These materials are typically white or pale-colored powders or granules, stable under ambient conditions, and used in textile, packaging, and plastic industries.

Dimethyl terephthalate is a colorless liquid ester derived from terephthalic acid and methanol. It serves as a key monomer for producing polyethylene terephthalate (PET) fibers and bottles. It is highly volatile, flammable, and commonly handled under controlled industrial conditions.

This category includes esters or anhydrides of tetrabromophthalic acid, benzene-1,2,4-tricarboxylic acid, isophthaloyldichloride (with ≤0.8% terephthaloyldichloride), naphthalene-1,4,5,8-tetracarboxylic acid, tetrachlorophthalic anhydride, and sodium 3,5-bis(methoxycarbonyl)benzenesulfonate. These are synthetic intermediates for specialty polymers and flame-retardant materials.

This residual category covers miscellaneous organic chemical substances not otherwise specified, including complex esters, derivatives, or unclassified intermediates. These may include specialty polymers, surfactants, or industrial catalysts not covered under specific HS codes.

Lactic acid and its salts and esters are chiral organic acids derived from renewable sources like corn or sugarcane. They are used in food, pharmaceuticals, and bioplastics. Salts include sodium lactate and calcium lactate; esters include lactyl acetate and lactyl alcohol.

Tartaric acid is a naturally occurring dicarboxylic acid found in grapes and other fruits. It is used as a food additive, antioxidant, and in wine stabilization. Pure tartaric acid is a white crystalline powder, often used in confectionery and as a leavening agent.

Salts and esters of tartaric acid are derived from tartaric acid and include potassium, sodium, and calcium tartrates. These are used as stabilizers, preservatives, and in food and pharmaceutical formulations. Esters like diethyl tartrate are used in chiral resolution processes.

Citric acid is a weak organic acid naturally found in citrus fruits. It is used as a flavor enhancer, preservative, and chelating agent. It exists as a white crystalline powder, often in hydrated forms, and is widely used in beverages, pharmaceuticals, and food processing.

Salts and esters of citric acid include sodium citrate, potassium citrate, and citrate esters. These are used as buffers, preservatives, and flavor enhancers. They are water-soluble, commonly found in food, cosmetics, and pharmaceuticals.

Gluconic acid and its salts and esters are derived from glucose. They are used as chelating agents, mild cleaning agents, and food additives. Gluconic acid is a white crystalline powder, and its salts (like sodium gluconate) are common in detergents and personal care products.

Benzilic acid, also known as 2,2-diphenyl-2-hydroxyacetic acid, is a crystalline organic compound used in pharmaceuticals and fine chemical synthesis. It is typically a white solid, often used as a precursor for benzil and other aromatic derivatives.

Chlorobenzilate (ISO) is a synthetic organic compound used as a precursor in the synthesis of certain pharmaceuticals and agrochemicals. It is typically a white crystalline solid, stable under ambient conditions, and handled in controlled industrial settings.

Cholic acid and deoxycholic acid, along with their salts and esters, are bile acids naturally occurring in the liver. They are used in pharmaceuticals, cosmetics, and as detergents. These are typically white or pale-yellow crystalline powders, often used in drug formulations.

2,2-Bis(hydroxymethyl)propionic acid is a colorless liquid used as a monomer in the synthesis of polyurethanes and polyesters. It is often used in coatings, adhesives, and polymers. It is highly reactive and typically handled under controlled conditions.

This residual category includes miscellaneous organic chemical substances not otherwise specified, such as unclassified esters, intermediates, or derivatives. These may include specialty polymers, surfactants, or industrial catalysts not covered under specific HS codes.

Salicylic acid and its salts are used in pharmaceuticals, cosmetics, and as antiseptics. Salts include sodium salicylate and aspirin precursors. It is typically a white crystalline powder, used in topical treatments and as a precursor to NSAIDs.

o-Acetylsalicylic acid (aspirin) and its salts and esters are widely used as analgesics and anti-inflammatory agents. Salts include sodium and potassium acetylsalicylates. These are typically white crystalline powders, used in pharmaceutical formulations.

Other esters of salicylic acid and their salts include esters like methyl and ethyl salicylates, used in perfumes, pharmaceuticals, and as antiseptics. These are typically liquids or solids with aromatic odors, used in topical and oral formulations.

This residual category covers miscellaneous organic chemical substances not otherwise specified, including complex esters, derivatives, or unclassified intermediates. These may include specialty polymers, surfactants, or industrial catalysts not covered under specific HS codes.

Carboxylic acids with aldehyde or ketone functional groups, excluding other oxygen-containing groups, along with their anhydrides, halides, peroxides, and derivatives. These are used in synthetic chemistry, pharmaceuticals, and as intermediates for polymers and solvents.

2,4,5-Trichlorophenoxyacetic acid (2,4,5-T) and its salts and esters are synthetic auxin herbicides. They are typically white crystalline powders or liquids, used in agriculture to control broadleaf weeds. Salts include sodium and calcium forms.

2,6-Dimethoxybenzoic acid, dicamba (ISO), and sodium phenoxyacetate are synthetic herbicides. They are typically white crystalline solids or liquids, used in agriculture to control broadleaf weeds. Sodium phenoxyacetate is a salt form of phenoxyacetic acid.

This residual category covers miscellaneous organic chemical substances not otherwise specified, including complex esters, derivatives, or unclassified intermediates. These may include specialty polymers, surfactants, or industrial catalysts not covered under specific HS codes.

Tris(2,3-dibromopropyl) phosphate is a flame-retardant additive used in plastics, textiles, and electronic components. It is typically a viscous liquid or solid, stable under ambient conditions, and used to improve fire resistance in polymers.

2,2-Methylenbis O.L. refers to other unspecified organic chemicals, including derivatives or unclassified compounds. These may include specialty polymers, surfactants, or industrial catalysts not covered under specific HS codes.

Parathion (ISO) and parathion-methyl (ISO) are organophosphate insecticides. They are typically colorless to pale-yellow liquids or solids, used in agriculture and pest control. These compounds are highly toxic and require careful handling.

Fenitrothion O.L. refers to other unspecified organophosphate compounds, including derivatives or unclassified insecticides. These are typically liquids or solids used in agricultural or industrial pest control applications.

Dimethyl phosphite is an organophosphorus compound used as a flame retardant and plasticizer. It is typically a colorless liquid, used in synthetic polymers and as a precursor for other phosphorus-containing chemicals.

Diethyl phosphite is an organophosphorus compound used as a flame retardant and plasticizer. It is typically a colorless liquid, used in synthetic polymers and as a precursor for other phosphorus-containing chemicals.

Trimethyl phosphite is an organophosphorus compound used as a flame retardant and plasticizer. It is typically a colorless liquid, used in synthetic polymers and as a precursor for other phosphorus-containing chemicals.

Triethyl phosphite is an organophosphorus compound used as a flame retardant and plasticizer. It is typically a colorless liquid, used in synthetic polymers and as a precursor for other phosphorus-containing chemicals.

This residual category covers miscellaneous organophosphorus compounds not otherwise specified, including derivatives or unclassified chemicals. These may include specialty polymers, surfactants, or industrial catalysts not covered under specific HS codes.

Endosulfan (ISO) is an organochlorine insecticide used in agriculture. It is typically a white crystalline solid or colorless liquid, used to control pests on crops. It is highly toxic and requires careful handling.

Sulfuric and carbonic esters, along with their salts and derivatives such as halogenated, sulfonated, nitrated, or nitrosated forms. These are used in pharmaceuticals, agrochemicals, and as solvents or intermediates in chemical synthesis.

This residual category covers miscellaneous organic chemical substances not otherwise specified, including complex esters, derivatives, or unclassified intermediates. These may include specialty polymers, surfactants, or industrial catalysts not covered under specific HS codes.

Methylamine, dimethylamine, trimethylamine, and their salts are organic amines used as intermediates in pharmaceuticals, agrochemicals, and surfactants. They are typically colorless gases or liquids, and their salts are crystalline solids.

2-(N,N-Dimethylamino)ethylchloride hydrochloride is a quaternary ammonium compound used as a surfactant or in pharmaceutical intermediates. It is typically a white crystalline solid, used in formulations requiring surface-active properties.

2-(N,N-Diethylamino)ethylchloride hydrochloride is a quaternary ammonium compound used as a surfactant or in pharmaceutical intermediates. It is typically a white crystalline solid, used in formulations requiring surface-active properties.

2-(N,N-Diisopropylamino)ethylchloride hydrochloride is a quaternary ammonium compound used as a surfactant or in pharmaceutical intermediates. It is typically a white crystalline solid, used in formulations requiring surface-active properties.

1,1,3,3-Tetramethylbutylamine is a tertiary amine used as a solvent, surfactant, or in pharmaceutical intermediates. It is typically a colorless liquid, used in formulations requiring surface-active properties.

Diethylamine and its salts are organic compounds used in industrial applications, such as pharmaceuticals, agrochemicals, and polymer production. Typically appearing as colorless liquids or solids, they are basic amines with moderate reactivity and are often handled under controlled conditions for safety.

This category covers miscellaneous amines and their salts not otherwise specified, including specialty chemicals, intermediates, or derivatives used in manufacturing, research, or industrial processes. These substances may vary in physical form and application but are generally not classified under more specific codes.

Ethylenediamine and its salts are diamines used as reducing agents, corrosion inhibitors, and in the synthesis of polymers and pharmaceuticals. They are typically colorless, viscous liquids, and are widely used in industrial cleaning, textile processing, and as precursors to polyurethanes and epoxy resins.

Hexamethylenediamine and its salts are diamines used primarily in nylon-6,6 production, as well as in pharmaceuticals and coatings. These compounds are typically crystalline solids or viscous liquids and are often handled in controlled environments due to their reactivity and potential toxicity.

This category includes unspecified amines and salts not falling under more specific codes. These may include industrial intermediates, specialty chemicals, or byproducts from chemical synthesis. Applications vary widely and may include pharmaceuticals, agrochemicals, or polymer precursors.

Cyclohexylamine and cyclohexyldimethylamine and their salts are amines used in pharmaceuticals, agrochemicals, and as intermediates in polymer synthesis. Typically appearing as liquids or solids, they are used in the production of herbicides, corrosion inhibitors, and as precursors to polyurethanes and epoxy resins.

1,3-Diaminocyclohexane, also known as cyclohex-1,3-ylenediamine, is a cyclic diamine used in the synthesis of nylon-6,6 and other polymers. It is typically a colorless to pale yellow liquid or solid and is employed as a monomer or intermediate in industrial chemical manufacturing.

This category covers unspecified amines and salts not falling under more specific codes. These may include industrial intermediates, specialty chemicals, or byproducts from chemical synthesis. Applications vary widely and may include pharmaceuticals, agrochemicals, or polymer precursors.

Aniline and its salts are aromatic amines used in dye manufacturing, pharmaceuticals, and agrochemicals. Typically appearing as colorless to pale yellow liquids or solids, they are key intermediates in the production of azo dyes, rubber chemicals, and various organic compounds.

Aniline derivatives and their salts include compounds such as substituted anilines used in pharmaceuticals, agrochemicals, and dyes. These may appear as liquids or solids and are typically employed in the synthesis of drugs, pigments, and as intermediates in polymer and fine chemical production.

Toluidines and their derivatives are aromatic amines used in dye and pigment production, pharmaceuticals, and agrochemicals. Typically appearing as colorless to pale yellow liquids or solids, they are intermediates in the synthesis of azo dyes, rubber chemicals, and various organic compounds.

Diphenylamine and its derivatives are aromatic amines used in the synthesis of antioxidants, pharmaceuticals, and agrochemicals. Typically appearing as colorless to pale yellow liquids or solids, they are employed as intermediates in the production of rubber chemicals and fine chemicals.

1-Naphthylamine and 2-naphthylamine and their derivatives are aromatic amines used in dye manufacturing, pharmaceuticals, and agrochemicals. Typically appearing as colorless to pale yellow liquids or solids, they are intermediates in the synthesis of azo dyes, rubber chemicals, and various organic compounds.

Amphetamines and their salts are central nervous system stimulants used in medicine and illicit drugs. These compounds may appear as colorless or white crystalline solids or liquids and are used in the treatment of obesity and narcolepsy, though they are strictly regulated due to their potential for abuse.

This category covers unspecified amines and salts not falling under more specific codes. These may include industrial intermediates, specialty chemicals, or byproducts from chemical synthesis. Applications vary widely and may include pharmaceuticals, agrochemicals, or polymer precursors.

m-Phenylenediamine, of at least 99% purity, containing limited impurities such as water, o-phenylenediamine, and p-phenylenediamine, is used in dyeing, printing, and rubber manufacturing. It is typically a pale yellow or colorless solid or liquid and is a key component in azo dye synthesis.

This category covers unspecified amines and salts not falling under more specific codes. These may include industrial intermediates, specialty chemicals, or byproducts from chemical synthesis. Applications vary widely and may include pharmaceuticals, agrochemicals, or polymer precursors.

This category covers unspecified amines and salts not falling under more specific codes. These may include industrial intermediates, specialty chemicals, or byproducts from chemical synthesis. Applications vary widely and may include pharmaceuticals, agrochemicals, or polymer precursors.

These compounds—m-phenylenebis(methylamine), 2,2'-dichloro-4,4'-methylenedianiline, 4,4'-bi-o-toluidine, and 1,8-naphthylenediamine—are aromatic amines used in dye, rubber, and pharmaceutical manufacturing. They are typically colorless to pale yellow liquids or solids and are intermediates in azo dye and polymer synthesis.

This category covers unspecified amines and salts not falling under more specific codes. These may include industrial intermediates, specialty chemicals, or byproducts from chemical synthesis. Applications vary widely and may include pharmaceuticals, agrochemicals, or polymer precursors.

Monoethanolamine and its salts are primary amines used in pharmaceuticals, detergents, and corrosion inhibitors. Typically appearing as colorless, viscous liquids or solids, they are key intermediates in the production of surfactants, antifreeze, and industrial cleaning agents.

Diethanolamine and its salts are secondary amines used in pharmaceuticals, detergents, and corrosion inhibitors. Typically appearing as colorless, viscous liquids or solids, they are key intermediates in the production of surfactants, antifreeze, and industrial cleaning agents.

Dextropropoxyphene (INN) and its salts are opioid analgesics used in the treatment of moderate pain. Typically appearing as white crystalline solids, they are used in pharmaceutical formulations and are regulated due to potential for abuse and dependency.

Triethanolamine is a tertiary amine used in pharmaceuticals, cosmetics, and industrial applications. Typically appearing as a colorless, viscous liquid or solid, it is used as a surfactant, emulsifier, and pH adjuster in various formulations.

Diethanolammonium perfluorooctane sulphonate is a surfactant used in industrial and pharmaceutical applications. It is typically a colorless, viscous liquid and is used as a wetting agent, dispersant, or in the formulation of pharmaceuticals and cleaning agents.

Methyldiethanolamine and ethyldiethanolamine are tertiary amines used in pharmaceuticals, detergents, and industrial applications. Typically appearing as colorless liquids or solids, they are used as surfactants, emulsifiers, and in the formulation of cleaning and personal care products.

2-(N,N-Diisopropylamino)ethanol is a tertiary amine used in pharmaceuticals and industrial applications. Typically appearing as a colorless liquid or solid, it is used as a solvent, surfactant, or in the synthesis of other chemicals.

This category covers unspecified amines and salts not falling under more specific codes. These may include industrial intermediates, specialty chemicals, or byproducts from chemical synthesis. Applications vary widely and may include pharmaceuticals, agrochemicals, or polymer precursors.

Aminohydroxynaphthalenesulphonic acids and their salts are used in dyeing, printing, and pharmaceutical applications. Typically appearing as colorless to pale yellow solids or liquids, they are intermediates in the synthesis of azo dyes and pharmaceuticals.

This category covers unspecified amines and salts not falling under more specific codes. These may include industrial intermediates, specialty chemicals, or byproducts from chemical synthesis. Applications vary widely and may include pharmaceuticals, agrochemicals, or polymer precursors.

Amfepramone (INN), methadone (INN), and normethadone (INN) and their salts are opioid analgesics and central nervous system depressants. Typically appearing as white crystalline solids, they are used in the treatment of pain and addiction, though they are strictly regulated due to potential for abuse.

This category covers unspecified salts of amines not falling under more specific codes. These may include industrial intermediates, specialty chemicals, or byproducts from chemical synthesis. Applications vary widely and may include pharmaceuticals, agrochemicals, or polymer precursors.

Lysine and its esters and salts are essential amino acids used in pharmaceuticals, food supplements, and animal feed. Typically appearing as white crystalline solids, they are key components in protein synthesis and are used in the formulation of nutritional supplements.

Glutamic acid and its salts are amino acids used in pharmaceuticals, food additives, and animal feed. Typically appearing as white crystalline solids, they are key components in protein synthesis and are used in the formulation of nutritional supplements and flavor enhancers.

Anthranilic acid and its salts are aromatic carboxylic acids used in dyeing, pharmaceuticals, and agrochemicals. Typically appearing as white crystalline solids, they are intermediates in the synthesis of azo dyes, pharmaceuticals, and other organic compounds.

Tilidine (INN) and its salts are opioid analgesics used in the treatment of pain. Typically appearing as white crystalline solids, they are used in pharmaceutical formulations and are regulated due to potential for abuse and dependency.

beta-Alanine is a non-essential amino acid used in pharmaceuticals, nutritional supplements, and cosmetics. Typically appearing as a white crystalline solid, it is used as a precursor to pantothenic acid and in the formulation of energy drinks and supplements.

This category covers unspecified amines and salts not falling under more specific codes. These may include industrial intermediates, specialty chemicals, or byproducts from chemical synthesis. Applications vary widely and may include pharmaceuticals, agrochemicals, or polymer precursors.

Amino-alcohol-phenols, amino-acid-phenols, and other amino-compounds with oxygen-containing functional groups are used in pharmaceuticals, agrochemicals, and industrial applications. These may appear as liquids or solids and are used as intermediates in the synthesis of dyes, drugs, and fine chemicals.

Choline and its salts are quaternary ammonium compounds used in pharmaceuticals, nutritional supplements, and animal feed. Typically appearing as white crystalline solids, they are essential for cell membrane integrity and are used in the formulation of nutritional supplements and pharmaceuticals.

Lecithins and other phosphoaminolipids are naturally occurring phospholipids, primarily derived from soybeans or eggs. They are used as emulsifiers in food, pharmaceuticals, and cosmetics. These compounds contain phosphorus and nitrogen and are essential for cell membrane structure. Common forms include liquid or powder, often refined for industrial applications.

Tetraethylammonium perfluorooctane sulphonate is a synthetic surfactant and fluorinated compound. It is used in specialized industrial applications, including firefighting foams and coatings. Its structure features a quaternary ammonium group with a perfluorinated chain, giving it high surface activity and chemical stability. It is typically supplied as a clear liquid or concentrated solution.

Didecyldimethylammonium perfluorooctane sulphonate is a cationic surfactant derived from perfluorooctane sulphonate. It is used in industrial cleaning agents and as a dispersant in specialty formulations. Its structure includes two decyl chains and a quaternary ammonium headgroup, enhancing its solubility and compatibility with polar solvents. Typically supplied as a liquid or solid salt.

Other chemical substances not specified elsewhere in Chapter 29.23 include miscellaneous organic compounds, often used as intermediates or specialty reagents. These may be synthetic or natural, with varied molecular structures. Examples include polymers, alcohols, or derivatives not covered in more specific codes. Typically supplied in pure or concentrated forms.

Meprobamate, also known as meprobam (INN), is a central nervous system depressant and sedative-hypnotic drug. It is used in the treatment of anxiety and insomnia. Chemically, it is a carbamate derivative. Typically supplied as white crystalline powder or tablets, it is stable under controlled storage conditions and requires careful handling due to its psychoactive effects.

Fluoroacetamide, monocrotophos, and phosphamidon are organophosphorus insecticides. Fluoroacetamide is a toxic compound used in industrial or research settings, while monocrotophos and phosphamidon are active ingredients in agricultural pesticides. These compounds are typically supplied as concentrated liquids or powders, with high toxicity requiring strict safety protocols during handling.

Other chemical substances not specified elsewhere in Chapter 29.24. These may include miscellaneous organic or inorganic compounds used as intermediates, reagents, or specialty chemicals. Examples include alcohols, esters, or salts not covered under more specific codes. Typically supplied in pure or concentrated forms, often for industrial or research use.

Ureines and their derivatives, including salts, are nitrogen-containing organic compounds. These are used as intermediates in the synthesis of pharmaceuticals, agrochemicals, and dyes. Examples include urea, thiourea, or substituted urea derivatives. Typically supplied as powders or liquids, they are stable under ambient conditions and require careful storage to prevent hydrolysis.

2-Acetamidobenzoic acid (N-acetylanthranilic acid) and its salts are aromatic carboxylic acids. They are used as intermediates in the synthesis of dyes, pharmaceuticals, and perfumes. The compound is typically supplied as a white crystalline solid or as its sodium or potassium salts. Stable under controlled conditions, it requires protection from moisture and light.

Ethinamate, also known as ethinam (INN), is a synthetic compound used in the pharmaceutical industry. It is a derivative of ethinyl estradiol and is used in the production of oral contraceptives or hormone therapies. Typically supplied as a white crystalline powder, it is sensitive to light and moisture and requires storage under controlled conditions.

Alachlor is a pre-emergent herbicide used in agriculture to control broadleaf weeds. It is a chlorinated aromatic compound and is typically supplied as a clear liquid or granular formulation. It is highly effective in cereal crops and requires careful application to avoid environmental contamination. It is not intended for direct human consumption.

Lidocaine, also known as lidocaine (INN), is a local anesthetic used in medical and dental procedures. It is a benzocaine derivative and is typically supplied as a white crystalline powder or solution. It is highly soluble in water and is used for topical, injectable, or inhalation applications. Requires careful handling due to its potential toxicity if misused.

Other chemical substances not specified elsewhere in Chapter 29.24. These include miscellaneous organic or inorganic compounds used as intermediates, reagents, or specialty chemicals. Examples may include alcohols, esters, or salts not covered under more specific codes. Typically supplied in pure or concentrated forms, often for industrial or research use.

Saccharin and its salts are artificial sweeteners used in food, pharmaceuticals, and cosmetics. Saccharin is a white crystalline compound with high sweetness potency, typically 300–500 times that of sucrose. It is stable under a wide range of conditions and is often supplied as a powder or liquid concentrate. Used in low-calorie products and pharmaceutical formulations.

Glutethimide, also known as glutethimide (INN), is a sedative-hypnotic drug used for sleep disorders. It is a benzodiazepine derivative and is typically supplied as a white crystalline powder or tablets. It is highly effective for short-term insomnia but has potential for dependence. Requires careful storage and controlled distribution due to its psychoactive properties.

3,3',4,4',5,5',6,6'-Octabromo-N,N'-ethylenediphthalimide and N,N'-ethylenebis(4,5-dibromohexahydro-3,6-methanophthalimide) are brominated aromatic compounds used as flame retardants or intermediates in polymer synthesis. These compounds are typically supplied as white crystalline powders or granules. They are stable under ambient conditions but require careful handling due to potential toxicity.

Other chemical substances not specified elsewhere in Chapter 29.25. These include miscellaneous organic or inorganic compounds used as intermediates, reagents, or specialty chemicals. Examples may include alcohols, esters, or salts not covered under more specific codes. Typically supplied in pure or concentrated forms, often for industrial or research use.

Chlordimeform, also known as chlordimeform (ISO), is a synthetic compound used as an insecticide or intermediate in chemical synthesis. It is a chlorinated aromatic compound and is typically supplied as a clear liquid or granular formulation. It is not intended for direct human consumption and requires careful handling due to its potential toxicity.

Dicyclohexylcarbodiimide and other unspecified chemicals. These compounds are used as coupling agents in peptide synthesis or as intermediates in chemical reactions. They are typically supplied as white crystalline powders or liquids. Stable under controlled conditions, they require careful handling due to potential reactivity or toxicity.

Acrylonitrile is a colorless liquid with a characteristic odor, used as a monomer in the production of acrylic fibers, resins, and plastics. It is highly reactive and requires careful handling. Typically supplied as a pure liquid or in solution form, it is stable under controlled conditions and is a key component in industrial polymer synthesis.

1-Cyanoguanidine, also known as dicyandiamide, is a nitrogen-rich organic compound used as a curing agent in resins and as a precursor for explosives. It is typically supplied as a white crystalline powder or granules. It is stable under ambient conditions but requires careful handling due to its potential reactivity and toxicity.

Fenproporex and methadone intermediate (4-cyano-2-dimethylamino-4,4-diphenylbutane) are synthetic compounds used in pharmaceuticals or as intermediates. Fenproporex is a sedative-hypnotic drug, while the methadone intermediate is used in the synthesis of methadone. These are typically supplied as white crystalline powders or liquids. Requires careful handling due to potential toxicity.

Alpha-phenylacetoacetonitrile is a synthetic compound used as an intermediate in the production of pharmaceuticals or dyes. It is a colorless liquid with a characteristic odor. Typically supplied as a pure liquid or solution, it is stable under controlled conditions and requires careful handling due to its potential reactivity or toxicity.

Isophthalonitrile is a synthetic aromatic nitrile used as an intermediate in the synthesis of dyes, resins, or pharmaceuticals. It is typically supplied as a white crystalline powder or liquid. It is stable under ambient conditions but requires careful handling due to potential reactivity or toxicity.

Other chemical substances not specified elsewhere in Chapter 29.26. These include miscellaneous organic or inorganic compounds used as intermediates, reagents, or specialty chemicals. Examples may include alcohols, esters, or salts not covered under more specific codes. Typically supplied in pure or concentrated forms, often for industrial or research use.

Diazo-, azo-, and azoxy-compounds are organic compounds containing nitrogen-oxygen linkages. They are used in dyes, pigments, and photographic chemicals. These compounds are typically supplied as powders or solutions. They are stable under controlled conditions but require careful handling due to potential toxicity or reactivity.

N,N-Bis(2-methoxyethyl)hydroxylamine is a synthetic organic compound used as an intermediate in the synthesis of pharmaceuticals or dyes. It is typically supplied as a white crystalline powder or liquid. It is stable under ambient conditions but requires careful handling due to potential reactivity or toxicity.

Other chemical substances not specified elsewhere in Chapter 29.28. These include miscellaneous organic or inorganic compounds used as intermediates, reagents, or specialty chemicals. Examples may include alcohols, esters, or salts not covered under more specific codes. Typically supplied in pure or concentrated forms, often for industrial or research use.

Isocyanates are reactive organic compounds used in the production of polyurethane foams, coatings, and adhesives. They are typically supplied as clear liquids or concentrated solutions. These compounds are highly reactive and require careful handling due to potential toxicity or skin irritation. They are essential in industrial manufacturing and construction applications.

Other chemical substances not specified elsewhere in Chapter 29.29. These include miscellaneous organic or inorganic compounds used as intermediates, reagents, or specialty chemicals. Examples may include alcohols, esters, or salts not covered under more specific codes. Typically supplied in pure or concentrated forms, often for industrial or research use.

2-(N,N-Dimethylamino)ethanethiol is a synthetic thiol compound used as an intermediate in pharmaceuticals or dyes. It is typically supplied as a clear liquid or concentrated solution. It is stable under controlled conditions but requires careful handling due to potential toxicity or reactivity.

Thiocarbamates and dithiocarbamates are organic compounds used as pesticides or intermediates in chemical synthesis. They are typically supplied as powders or liquids. These compounds are stable under ambient conditions but require careful handling due to potential toxicity or environmental impact.

Thiuram mono-, di-, or tetrasulphides are sulfur-containing organic compounds used as intermediates in the synthesis of rubber chemicals or pesticides. They are typically supplied as white crystalline powders or granules. They are stable under controlled conditions but require careful handling due to potential toxicity or reactivity.

Methionine, also known as methionine (INN), is an essential amino acid used in animal feed, nutritional supplements, and pharmaceuticals. It is typically supplied as a white crystalline powder or granules. It is stable under ambient conditions and is vital for protein synthesis in biological systems.

Other chemical substances not specified elsewhere in Chapter 29.30. These include miscellaneous organic or inorganic compounds used as intermediates, reagents, or specialty chemicals. Examples may include alcohols, esters, or salts not covered under more specific codes. Typically supplied in pure or concentrated forms, often for industrial or research use.

2-(N,N-Diethylamino)ethanethiol is a synthetic thiol compound used as an intermediate in pharmaceuticals or dyes. It is typically supplied as a clear liquid or concentrated solution. It is stable under controlled conditions but requires careful handling due to potential toxicity or reactivity.

Bis(2-hydroxyethyl)sulfide, also known as thiodiglycol (INN), is a synthetic compound used as a solvent or intermediate in the production of pharmaceuticals or polymers. It is typically supplied as a clear liquid or concentrated solution. It is stable under ambient conditions but requires careful handling due to potential toxicity or reactivity.

Aldicarb, captafol, and methamidophos are organophosphorus insecticides used in agriculture. These compounds are typically supplied as concentrated liquids or granular formulations. They are highly effective against pests but require careful handling due to their potential toxicity and environmental impact.

Cysteine and cystine are sulfur-containing amino acids used in nutritional supplements, pharmaceuticals, and biochemical research. Cysteine is typically supplied as a white crystalline powder or granules. It is stable under ambient conditions and is essential for protein synthesis and antioxidant functions in biological systems.

Derivatives of cysteine or cystine are synthetic compounds used as intermediates in pharmaceuticals or biochemical research. These compounds are typically supplied as powders or liquids. They are stable under controlled conditions but require careful handling due to potential toxicity or reactivity.

DL-2-hydroxy-4-(methylthio)butyric acid, a chiral organic compound with a hydroxyl and thioether functional group, used primarily as a chemical intermediate in pharmaceutical synthesis and as a flavoring agent in food additives.

2,2'-Thiodiethylbis[3-(3,5-di-tert-butyl-4-hydroxyphenyl)propionate], a synthetic antioxidant compound derived from phenolic groups and thioether linkages, commonly used in polymer stabilization and as a corrosion inhibitor in industrial applications.

A mixture of isomeric compounds: 4-methyl-2,6-bis(methylthio)-m-phenylenediamine and 2-methyl-4,6-bis(methylthio)-m-phenylenediamine, both containing methylthio groups and aromatic diamine structures, used as intermediates in dye and polymer synthesis.
Miscellaneous chemical substances not classified elsewhere under Chapter 29, including unspecified organic and inorganic compounds used in industrial, pharmaceutical, or research contexts.

Tetramethyl lead and tetraethyl lead, organolead compounds with high toxicity, historically used as antiknock additives in gasoline and as pesticides, now largely restricted due to environmental and health hazards.

Tributyltin compounds, organotin chemicals containing three butyl groups attached to tin, historically used as biocides, antifouling agents, and plastic stabilizers, now restricted due to environmental persistence and toxicity.
Dimethyl methylphosphonate, an organophosphorus compound with methyl groups attached to phosphorus, used as a precursor in flame retardants, pesticides, and chemical synthesis.
Dimethyl propylphosphonate, a phosphonate ester with methyl and propyl groups, employed in chemical synthesis, as a flame retardant additive, and in pesticide formulations.
Diethyl ethylphosphonate, an organophosphorus compound with ethyl groups, used as a synthetic intermediate in agrochemicals, pharmaceuticals, and flame-retardant materials.
Methylphosphonic acid, a simple organophosphorus acid with one methyl group, used as a precursor in flame retardants, corrosion inhibitors, and as a component in pesticide formulations.
A 1:1 salt formed between methylphosphonic acid and (aminoiminomethyl)urea, used as a corrosion inhibitor and in polymer stabilization applications.
2,4,6-Tripropyl-1,3,5,2,4,6-trioxatriphosphinane 2,4,6-trioxide, a complex organophosphorus compound with three propyl groups, used as a flame retardant and in polymer modification.
A complex organophosphorus compound with an ethyl, methyl, and phosphonate moiety, used as a flame retardant and in the synthesis of specialty polymers and coatings.

3,9-Dimethyl-2,4,8,10-tetraoxa-3,9-diphosphaspiro[5.5]undecane 3,9-dioxide, a cyclic organophosphorus compound with phosphorus and oxygen atoms, used in flame retardant formulations and polymer stabilization.
Sodium 3-(trihydroxysilyl)propyl methylphosphonate, a silyl-terminated phosphonate salt used in surface modification of materials, particularly in coatings and adhesives for ceramics and glass.
Bis[(5-ethyl-2-methyl-2-oxido-1,3,2-dioxaphosphinan-5-yl)methyl] methylphosphonate, a complex organophosphorus compound used as a flame retardant and in polymer stabilization applications.
Etidronic acid (INN) and its salts, a diphosphonic acid used as a chelating agent in medical applications, particularly in bone mineralization and metal ion sequestration.

A group of complex organophosphorus compounds including nitrilotrimethylenetrisphosphonic acid and similar derivatives, used as chelating agents and corrosion inhibitors in industrial and pharmaceutical applications.
Miscellaneous chemical substances not classified elsewhere under Chapter 29, including unspecified organophosphorus compounds used in industrial, pharmaceutical, or research contexts.
Methylphosphonic dichloride, a chlorine-containing organophosphorus compound used as a precursor in flame retardants, pesticides, and chemical synthesis.
Propylphosphonic dichloride, an organophosphorus compound with a propyl group, used as a chemical intermediate in flame retardant and pesticide formulations.
O-(3-chloropropyl) O-[4-nitro-3-(trifluoromethyl)phenyl] methylphosphonothionate, a phosphonothionate compound with chloro, nitro, and trifluoromethyl substituents, used as a pesticide or herbicide.
Trichlorfon (ISO), an organophosphorus insecticide used to control pests in agriculture, horticulture, and public health applications.
Methylphosphonoyl difluoride, a fluorinated organophosphorus compound used as a chemical intermediate in pesticide and flame-retardant synthesis.
Miscellaneous chemical substances not classified elsewhere under Chapter 29, including unspecified organophosphorus compounds used in industrial, pharmaceutical, or research contexts.
Miscellaneous chemical substances not classified elsewhere under Chapter 29, including unspecified inorganic or organic compounds used in industrial, pharmaceutical, or research contexts.
Tetrahydrofuran, a cyclic ether compound used as a solvent in chemical synthesis and as a precursor to polyurethanes and other polymers.
2-Furaldehyde (furfuraldehyde), a colorless liquid aldehyde derived from furan, used as a flavoring agent, solvent, and in the production of resins and pharmaceuticals.
Furfuryl alcohol and tetrahydrofurfuryl alcohol, alcohols derived from furan, used as resins in foundry applications, solvents, and as intermediates in polymer synthesis.
Sucralose, a synthetic sweetener with high sweetness and low caloric content, used as a sugar substitute in food and beverage products.
Miscellaneous chemical substances not classified elsewhere under Chapter 29, including unspecified organic compounds used in industrial, pharmaceutical, or research contexts.
Phenolphthalein, a pH indicator dye used in laboratories and educational settings, along with other complex organic compounds such as naphthyl derivatives used in dyes and pharmaceuticals.
Gamma-butyrolactone, a cyclic ester used as a solvent, in the synthesis of pharmaceuticals, and as a precursor to polymers such as polyurethanes and polyesters.
Miscellaneous chemical substances not classified elsewhere under Chapter 29, including unspecified organic compounds used in industrial, pharmaceutical, or research contexts.
Isosafrol, a natural compound derived from plants, used as a flavoring agent and in the synthesis of pharmaceuticals and perfumes.
1-(1,3-Benzodioxol-5-yl)propan-2-one, an aromatic ketone used as a fragrance ingredient and in the synthesis of pharmaceuticals and perfumes.
Piperonal, an aromatic ketone with a piperonal structure, used as a fragrance ingredient and in the synthesis of pharmaceuticals and perfumes.
Safrol, an aromatic compound derived from plants, used as a flavoring agent and in the synthesis of pharmaceuticals and perfumes.
Tetrahydrocannabinol (THC) and its isomers, psychoactive compounds derived from Cannabis sativa, used in pharmaceuticals and as recreational substances.
Carbofuran (ISO), an organophosphorus insecticide used to control pests in agriculture, horticulture, and public health applications.

Other chemical substances not specified elsewhere in Chapter 29, including miscellaneous organic and inorganic compounds not falling under specific categories, typically used in industrial, pharmaceutical, or research applications.
Propyphenazone (INN), a white crystalline compound used primarily as a non-narcotic analgesic and antipyretic, often formulated for oral administration in pharmaceutical preparations.
Other chemical substances not specified elsewhere in Chapter 29, including miscellaneous non-narcotic analgesics, antipyretics, or related compounds not classified under specific INN codes.
Phenylbutazone (INN), a white crystalline compound used as an anti-inflammatory and analgesic, commonly administered to livestock and occasionally in human medicine for rheumatoid arthritis.
Other chemical substances not specified elsewhere in Chapter 29, including miscellaneous anti-inflammatory, analgesic, or related compounds not classified under specific INN codes.
Hydantoin and its derivatives, including compounds like phenytoin, used as anticonvulsants and antiseizure medications, typically formulated as crystalline solids for pharmaceutical use.

Naphazoline hydrochloride and naphazoline nitrate (INNM), used as topical vasoconstrictors for nasal congestion; phentolamine (INN), a vasodilator and alpha-blocker; tolazoline hydrochloride (INNM), used for antihypertensive and anti-inflammatory purposes.
Other chemical substances not specified elsewhere in Chapter 29, including miscellaneous sympathomimetic or antihypertensive agents not classified under specific INN codes.
Pyridine and its salts, including pyridine hydrochloride or pyridine sulfate, used as solvents, intermediates in pharmaceuticals, or corrosion inhibitors in industrial applications.
Piperidine and its salts, including piperidine hydrochloride, used as solvents, pharmaceutical intermediates, or corrosion inhibitors in industrial and chemical processes.

Alfentanil (INN), aneridin (INN), bezitramid (INN), bromazepam (INN), carfentanil (INN), difenoxin (INN), diphenoxylate (INN), dipipanon (INN), fentanyl (INN), ketobemidon (INN), methylphenidate (INN), pentazocin (INN), pethidine (INN), pethidine melamine intermediate A, phencyclidine (INN) (PCP), phenoperidin (INN), pipradrol (INN), piritramid (INN), propiram (INN), remifentanil (INN) — a group of synthetic opioids, sedatives, or anxiolytics used in medical or veterinary settings, including controlled substances.
Other fentanyls and their derivatives, including compounds like fentanyl analogs or metabolites not classified under specific INN codes, often used as potent analgesics or research chemicals.
3-Quinuclidinol, a crystalline compound used as a pharmaceutical intermediate or in research applications, often involved in the synthesis of anticholinergic or CNS-active compounds.
4-Anilino-N-phenethylpiperidine (ANPP), a synthetic compound used as a pharmaceutical intermediate or research chemical, often involved in the synthesis of CNS-active or anticholinergic agents.
N-Phenethyl-4-piperidone (NPP), a synthetic compound used as a pharmaceutical intermediate or research chemical, often involved in the synthesis of CNS-active or anticholinergic agents.

Iproniazid (INN), a monoamine oxidase inhibitor (MAOI) used historically as an antidepressant; ketobemidone hydrochloride (INNM), an opioid analgesic; pyridostigmine bromide (INN), an anticholinesterase used for myasthenia gravis treatment.
2,3,5,6-Tetrachloropyridine, a chlorinated aromatic compound used as a chemical intermediate or in pesticide synthesis, often in agrochemical formulations.
3,6-Dichloropyridine-2-carboxylic acid, a chlorinated aromatic carboxylic acid used as a chemical intermediate in pharmaceutical or agrochemical synthesis.
2-Hydroxyethylammonium-3,6-dichloropyridine-2-carboxylate, a quaternary ammonium salt of a dichlorinated pyridine carboxylic acid, used as a chemical intermediate or in agrochemical formulations.
2-Butoxyethyl(3,5,6-trichloro-2-pyridyloxy)acetate, a chlorinated pyridine derivative used as a chemical intermediate or in agrochemical formulations.
3,5-Dichloro-2,4,6-trifluoropyridine, a fluorinated and chlorinated aromatic compound used as a chemical intermediate or in agrochemical synthesis.
Fluroxypyr (ISO), methyl ester — a synthetic herbicide used for selective weed control in turf and ornamental settings, typically applied as a liquid or granular formulation.
4-Methylpyridine, a substituted aromatic heterocycle used as a chemical intermediate in pharmaceuticals, agrochemicals, or as a solvent.

Other chemical substances not specified elsewhere in Chapter 29, including miscellaneous organic or inorganic compounds not falling under specific categories, typically used in industrial or research applications.
Levorphanol (INN) and its salts, including levorphanol hydrochloride, used as potent opioid analgesics for severe pain management, typically formulated as injectables or tablets.

Halogenated derivatives of quinoline and derivatives of quinolinecarboxylic acid, including compounds like chloroquinoline or quinolinecarboxylic acid esters, used in pharmaceuticals or agrochemicals.
Dextromethorphan (INN) and its salts, including dextromethorphan hydrobromide, used as a cough suppressant and analgesic, often formulated for oral administration.
Other chemical substances not specified elsewhere in Chapter 29, including miscellaneous quinoline derivatives or related compounds not classified under specific INN codes.
Malonylurea (barbituric acid) and its salts, including barbituric acid itself or its sodium or calcium salts, used as anticonvulsants or in pharmaceutical formulations.
Phenobarbital (INN), barbital (INN), and their salts, including phenobarbital sodium or calcium salts, used as sedatives, anticonvulsants, or anxiolytics in medical applications.
