Spades and shovels are hand-held digging tools with flat, broad blades for moving soil, sand, or loose materials. Typically made of steel or alloyed metals, they feature sturdy handles and are used in agriculture, construction, and landscaping for digging, scraping, or scooping.

Mattocks, picks, hoes, and rakes are hand tools used for digging, breaking up soil, or gathering materials. Mattocks have a flat blade and a pointed end for prying; picks are pointed for breaking hard surfaces; hoes for weeding; and rakes for spreading or gathering loose material. Made primarily of steel or alloyed metals.

Axes, billhooks, and similar hewing tools are hand-held implements with cutting edges for chopping, splitting, or cutting wood. Axes have a single cutting edge; billhooks have curved blades for cutting branches or shaping wood. Typically forged or cast steel, used in forestry and woodworking.

Secateurs and similar one-handed pruners and shears are hand tools for cutting small branches or stems. Includes poultry shears with sharp, pointed blades. Made of hardened steel, often with spring-loaded mechanisms, used in gardening, orchards, and poultry processing.

Hedge shears, two-handed pruning shears, and similar tools are hand-held cutting devices with two blades that open and close for trimming vegetation. Used in landscaping and agriculture, typically made of hardened steel, with handles designed for two-handed operation for precision cutting.

Other hand tools used in agriculture, horticulture, or forestry include scrapers, trowels, and small adzes. These tools are typically made of steel or alloyed metals and are designed for manual use in soil preparation, plant care, or wood processing. Not covered by other specific categories.

Handsaws are hand-held saws with toothed blades for cutting wood or other materials. Typically made of steel, with handles for grip and control. Used in woodworking, construction, and carpentry for making straight or curved cuts in timber or other materials.

Bandsaw blades are narrow, toothed blades that run between two wheels to cut materials. Used in woodworking, metalworking, and metal fabrication. Typically made of high-speed steel or carbide-tipped teeth, designed for continuous cutting of wood, metal, or plastic with precision.

Circular saw blades with steel cutting edges are designed for cutting wood, metal, or other materials. The steel teeth are hardened and ground for durability and sharpness. Used in construction, woodworking, and metalworking for making accurate, clean cuts in various materials.

Other saw blades, including spare parts or accessories, are not specifically categorized elsewhere. This includes blades for circular saws, bandsaws, or jigsaws, as well as replacement blades or blade holders. Typically made of steel or alloyed metals for cutting various materials.

Chainsaw blades are cutting tools designed for use with chainsaws. These blades are typically made of high-speed steel or carbide-tipped teeth and are designed to cut wood or other materials with high efficiency. Used in forestry, landscaping, and construction for cutting large logs or branches.

Straight saw blades specifically designed for cutting metal. These blades are made of hardened steel or alloyed metals with high-speed steel teeth for cutting through metal sheets or bars. Used in metal fabrication, construction, and manufacturing industries for precise metal cutting.

Saw blades designed for cutting metal, including circular saw blades, bandsaw blades, or jigsaws. Made of hardened steel or carbide-tipped teeth, these blades are used in metalworking, fabrication, and construction for cutting metal sheets, bars, or pipes with precision.

Saw blades designed for cutting materials other than metal, such as wood, plastic, or composite materials. These blades are typically made of high-speed steel or carbide-tipped teeth, designed for cutting wood or plastic with precision in woodworking, construction, or manufacturing.

Files, rasps, and similar tools are hand-held abrasive tools with cutting edges for smoothing, shaping, or finishing metal or wood. Typically made of hardened steel or alloyed metals, they are used in metalworking, woodworking, and mechanical repair for removing material or refining surfaces.

Pliers, pincers, tweezers, and similar hand tools are used for gripping, cutting, or bending materials. Made of hardened steel or alloyed metals, they are used in electrical work, mechanical repair, and precision tasks. Includes cutting pliers, needle-nose pliers, and wire cutters.

Metal-cutting shears and similar tools are hand-held cutting devices with two blades for cutting metal sheets or wires. Typically made of hardened steel or alloyed metals, they are used in metalworking, fabrication, and electrical work for cutting metal with precision and control.

Pipe-cutters, bolt croppers, perforating punches, and similar tools are hand-held implements for cutting or shaping metal pipes, bolts, or other materials. Made of hardened steel or alloyed metals, they are used in plumbing, mechanical repair, and construction for cutting or perforating metal components.

Non-adjustable spanners are hand tools with fixed-size jaws for tightening or loosening nuts and bolts. Typically made of hardened steel or alloyed metals, they are used in mechanical repair, construction, and maintenance for precise fastening of fasteners with specific sizes.

Adjustable spanners are hand tools with movable jaws that can be adjusted to fit different sizes of nuts and bolts. Typically made of hardened steel or alloyed metals, they are used in mechanical repair, construction, and maintenance for tightening or loosening fasteners of various sizes.

Interchangeable spanner sockets with or without handles are hand tools for tightening or loosening nuts and bolts. The sockets are designed to fit different sizes of fasteners and can be swapped for different applications. Typically made of hardened steel or alloyed metals for durability.

Drilling, threading, or tapping tools are hand-held implements for creating holes, cutting threads, or forming taps in metal or other materials. Typically made of hardened steel or alloyed metals, they are used in mechanical repair, construction, and manufacturing for creating precise holes or threads.

Hammers and sledge hammers are hand-held striking tools with heads for driving nails, breaking materials, or shaping metal. Typically made of hardened steel or alloyed metals, they are used in construction, mechanical repair, and metalworking for driving nails, breaking concrete, or shaping metal.

Planes, chisels, gouges, and similar cutting tools are hand-held implements for shaping or smoothing wood. Typically made of hardened steel or alloyed metals, they are used in woodworking, carpentry, and construction for cutting, planing, or shaping wood surfaces with precision.

Screwdrivers are hand-held tools with blades for turning screws. Typically made of hardened steel or alloyed metals, they are used in mechanical repair, construction, and manufacturing for tightening or loosening screws of various sizes and types.

Household tools are hand-held implements for general domestic use. Includes tools such as screwdrivers, pliers, wrenches, and other tools used for minor repairs or maintenance in homes. Typically made of hardened steel or alloyed metals for durability and precision.

Tools for masons, moulders, cement workers, plasterers, and painters are hand-held implements for construction and finishing work. Includes trowels, floaters, brushes, and other tools used for applying plaster, cement, paint, or other materials. Typically made of hardened steel or alloyed metals.

Other hand tools not specified elsewhere are hand-held implements for various applications. Includes tools for mechanical repair, construction, or maintenance. Typically made of hardened steel or alloyed metals, used for tasks requiring manual force or precision.

Blowlamps are hand-held or portable tools for producing a flame for heating, welding, or cutting. Typically made of metal with a fuel reservoir and nozzle, used in metalworking, construction, or repair for heating or melting materials.

Vices, clamps, and similar tools are hand-held or portable devices for holding or securing materials. Typically made of hardened steel or alloyed metals, they are used in mechanical repair, construction, and manufacturing for holding materials in place during operations.

Anvils, portable forges, and hand- or pedal-operated grinding wheels with frameworks are hand tools for shaping, cutting, or grinding materials. Typically made of hardened steel or alloyed metals, they are used in metalworking, construction, or repair for shaping metal or grinding materials.

Sets of articles from two or more subheadings under this heading are collections of hand tools or implements grouped for specific applications. Typically include tools from different categories such as saws, files, or pliers, designed for specific tasks or industries.

Tools of two or more of headings 8202 to 8205, put up in sets for retail sale are collections of hand tools grouped for specific applications or industries. Typically include saw blades, files, pliers, or other tools, packaged for retail sale to consumers or professionals.

Borers with working parts of cermets are hand-held tools for drilling or cutting materials. Cermets are composite materials combining ceramic and metal, providing high hardness and wear resistance. Used in metalworking, construction, or repair for drilling or cutting hard materials.

Borers with working parts of diamond or agglomerated diamond are hand-held tools for drilling or cutting materials. Diamond or agglomerated diamond provides extreme hardness and wear resistance. Used in metalworking, construction, or repair for drilling or cutting very hard materials.

Other borers are hand-held tools for drilling or cutting materials. Made of various materials such as steel, carbide, or other alloys, they are used in metalworking, construction, or repair for drilling or cutting materials not covered by other categories.

Borers with working parts of diamond or agglomerated diamond are hand-held tools for drilling or cutting materials. Diamond or agglomerated diamond provides extreme hardness and wear resistance. Used in metalworking, construction, or repair for drilling or cutting very hard materials.

Borers with working parts of other materials are hand-held tools for drilling or cutting materials. Made of various materials such as steel, carbide, or other alloys, they are used in metalworking, construction, or repair for drilling or cutting materials not covered by other categories.

Borers for working metal are hand-held tools for drilling or cutting metal. Typically made of hardened steel or carbide-tipped teeth, they are used in metalworking, construction, or repair for drilling holes or cutting metal with precision.

Other borers are hand-held tools for drilling or cutting materials. Made of various materials such as steel, carbide, or other alloys, they are used in metalworking, construction, or repair for drilling or cutting materials not covered by other categories.

Tools designed for cutting internal threads into metal or other materials, typically used in manufacturing or repair work. These tools are often made of hardened steel and come in various sizes for precision applications.

Tools for cutting external threads on cylindrical surfaces, commonly used in metalworking to secure components. These tools are usually made of high-speed steel or carbide for durability during threading operations.

Miscellaneous tools for cutting, threading, or drilling, not classified elsewhere in Chapter 82. These may include hand or machine tools for non-standard applications, often with specialized geometries or materials.

Drills with cutting edges made from diamond or agglomerated diamond, designed for cutting hard materials such as stone, ceramic, or glass. These are precision tools requiring specialized handling and cooling.

Drills specifically designed for drilling into masonry, concrete, or brick. These tools often have tungsten carbide tips or diamond-coated edges to withstand abrasive materials and high impact forces.

Drills made from cermets — composite materials combining ceramic and metallic components — for cutting hard metals or abrasive materials. These tools offer high heat resistance and durability for industrial applications.
Drills manufactured from high-speed steel, ideal for cutting metal with high efficiency and durability. These tools are commonly used in metalworking and construction for general-purpose drilling.

Drills made from materials other than diamond, cermets, or high-speed steel — such as tungsten carbide or sintered alloys — for specialized cutting applications in metal, stone, or composite materials.
Drills not classified under other specific categories in Chapter 82. These may include experimental or custom tools, or those with unconventional geometries or materials for niche applications.

Drills with cutting edges made from diamond or agglomerated diamond, designed for cutting hard materials such as stone, ceramic, or glass. These tools are precision tools requiring specialized handling and cooling.
Drills designed for metalworking, often with carbide or high-speed steel cutting edges. These tools are used in manufacturing to create holes or cut profiles in metal parts for assembly or machining.

Miscellaneous drills not classified under other specific categories. These may include hand tools, experimental tools, or tools with unconventional geometries or materials for specialized applications.

Drills specifically designed for metalworking, often with carbide or high-speed steel cutting edges. These tools are used in manufacturing to create holes or cut profiles in metal parts for assembly or machining.

Miscellaneous drills not classified under other specific categories. These may include hand tools, experimental tools, or tools with unconventional geometries or materials for specialized applications.

Drills made from cermets — composite materials combining ceramic and metallic components — for cutting hard metals or abrasive materials. These tools offer high heat resistance and durability for industrial applications.
Drills with shank-type designs, typically used in machine tools or industrial applications. These tools are designed for secure mounting and precise cutting in metal or other hard materials.
Miscellaneous drilling tools not classified elsewhere, including hand tools, experimental tools, or tools with unconventional geometries or materials for specialized applications.
Miscellaneous drills not classified under other specific categories. These may include experimental or custom tools, or those with unconventional geometries or materials for niche applications.

Drills made from cermets — composite materials combining ceramic and metallic components — for cutting hard metals or abrasive materials. These tools offer high heat resistance and durability for industrial applications.

Drills made from materials other than diamond, cermets, or high-speed steel — such as tungsten carbide or sintered alloys — for specialized cutting applications in metal, stone, or composite materials.
Miscellaneous drills not classified under other specific categories. These may include experimental or custom tools, or those with unconventional geometries or materials for niche applications.

Amboes with cutting edges made from diamond or agglomerated diamond, designed for cutting hard materials such as stone, ceramic, or glass. These tools are precision tools requiring specialized handling and cooling.

Screwdriver bits designed for use in screwdrivers or electric screwdrivers. These tools are used to drive screws into metal or other materials, often with specialized tip geometries for different screw types.

Gear-cutting tools used to cut gear teeth into metal or other materials. These tools are designed for precision cutting of gear profiles and may be used in machine tools or industrial gear manufacturing.
Drills designed for metalworking, often with carbide or high-speed steel cutting edges. These tools are used in manufacturing to create holes or cut profiles in metal parts for assembly or machining.

Miscellaneous tools not classified under other specific categories. These may include hand tools, experimental tools, or tools with unconventional geometries or materials for specialized applications.
Drills designed for metalworking, often with carbide or high-speed steel cutting edges. These tools are used in manufacturing to create holes or cut profiles in metal parts for assembly or machining.
Miscellaneous tools not classified under other specific categories. These may include experimental or custom tools, or those with unconventional geometries or materials for niche applications.
Machine tools designed for metalworking, including lathes, milling machines, or drill presses. These tools are used to cut, shape, or finish metal parts in industrial or manufacturing settings.
Machine tools designed for woodworking, including saws, planers, or routers. These tools are used to cut, shape, or finish wood for furniture, construction, or cabinetry applications.
Machine tools designed for use in kitchen appliances or food industry machinery, including parts for mixers, blenders, or food processors. These tools are used to process or prepare food materials.

Machine tools designed for agricultural, horticultural, or forestry machinery, including parts for tractors, harvesters, or wood chippers. These tools are used to cut, shape, or process plant materials.
Miscellaneous machine tools not classified under other specific categories. These may include experimental tools, custom tools, or tools with unconventional geometries for niche applications.

Indexable inserts used in machine tools for cutting, drilling, or milling. These inserts are replaceable and designed for high-speed metalworking applications, often with carbide or ceramic cutting edges.

Miscellaneous inserts or cutting tools not classified under other specific categories. These may include experimental tools, custom tools, or tools with unconventional geometries for niche applications.

Hand-operated mechanical appliances weighing 10 kg or less, used for preparing, conditioning, or serving food or drink. These include tools such as grinders, mixers, or dispensers for commercial or household use.
Sets of assorted tools or implements, typically used for general-purpose cutting, scraping, or piercing. These sets may include knives, scrapers, or other implements for household or industrial use.
Table knives with fixed blades, designed for cutting food on dining tables or in kitchens. These knives are typically made of stainless steel and have a fixed blade for safety and stability.
Knives with fixed blades, not classified as table knives. These may include utility knives, carving knives, or other fixed-blade tools for cutting or trimming materials.
Knives with blades other than fixed blades, such as folding knives, spring-loaded knives, or multi-blade tools. These are used for cutting, trimming, or handling materials in various applications.

Knives and blades, including single-edged or double-edged cutting tools, typically made of steel or other durable metals, designed for general cutting or trimming purposes, such as utility knives or precision blades used in industrial, household, or professional applications.

Handles for knives or blades, made of base metal such as steel, iron, or brass, designed to attach to or support cutting blades, often used in tools like kitchen knives, utility knives, or industrial cutting implements, and typically not finished with precious metals.

Safety razors with fixed blades, typically made of high-carbon steel or stainless steel, designed for close-shaving without replaceable blades, often used for personal grooming and manufactured with ergonomic handles and protective guards for safety.

Other parts or accessories for razors, such as blade guards, adjustment mechanisms, or cosmetic cases, not classified elsewhere, including components for electric or manual razors, typically made of plastic, metal, or composite materials for durability and function.

Safety razor blades, including blank strips of metal designed for cutting, typically made of high-carbon steel or stainless steel, used in manufacturing or as replacement blades for safety razors, and often supplied in standardized sizes for industrial or retail use.

Other parts for razors, such as blade holders, spring mechanisms, or protective covers, not classified elsewhere, typically made of metal or plastic, designed to support or protect the blade during use or storage, and often used in safety or disposable razors.

Scissors, tailors’ shears, and similar cutting instruments, typically made of stainless steel or high-carbon steel, designed for cutting fabric, paper, or other materials, and often featuring ergonomic handles and precision blades for professional or household use.

Paper knives, letter openers, erasing knives, pencil sharpeners, and their blades, typically made of metal or plastic, designed for cutting paper, opening envelopes, erasing pencil marks, or sharpening pencils, and often used in office, school, or home environments.

Manicure or pedicure instruments, including nail files, clippers, and cutting tools, typically made of metal or plastic, designed for shaping or trimming fingernails or toenails, and often sold as sets with multiple tools for personal grooming or salon use.

Other grooming or beauty tools not classified elsewhere, including cosmetic applicators, tweezers, or small cutting implements, typically made of metal or plastic, designed for personal hygiene or cosmetic use, and often used in beauty salons or at home.

Cufflinks or similar accessories plated with precious metals such as gold, silver, or platinum, typically used as fashion accessories for formal attire, and designed to fasten shirt cuffs, often made of base metal with a decorative precious metal finish.

Cufflinks or similar accessories made of stainless steel, designed for fastening shirt cuffs, typically used in formal or business attire, and often manufactured with polished or brushed finishes for durability and aesthetic appeal.

Other cufflinks or similar accessories not classified elsewhere, including decorative or functional items, typically made of base metal or composite materials, designed for fastening shirts or as fashion accessories, and often sold in sets or individually.

Cufflinks or similar accessories made of stainless steel, designed for fastening shirt cuffs, typically used in formal or business attire, and often manufactured with polished or brushed finishes for durability and aesthetic appeal.

Other cufflinks or similar accessories not classified elsewhere, including decorative or functional items, typically made of base metal or composite materials, designed for fastening shirts or as fashion accessories, and often sold in sets or individually.

Cufflinks or similar accessories plated with precious metals such as gold, silver, or platinum, typically used as fashion accessories for formal attire, and designed to fasten shirt cuffs, often made of base metal with a decorative precious metal finish.

Cufflinks or similar accessories made of stainless steel, designed for fastening shirt cuffs, typically used in formal or business attire, and often manufactured with polished or brushed finishes for durability and aesthetic appeal.

Other cufflinks or similar accessories not classified elsewhere, including decorative or functional items, typically made of base metal or composite materials, designed for fastening shirts or as fashion accessories, and often sold in sets or individually.

Padlocks designed for general security use, typically featuring a shackle and locking mechanism, often made of steel or brass, used to secure doors, gates, or containers, and commonly found in household, commercial, or industrial applications.

Locks specifically engineered for motor vehicles, including car doors, trunks, and glove compartments, often incorporating key-operated or keyless mechanisms, designed to withstand vehicle-specific stresses and environmental conditions.

Locks designed for furniture applications such as drawers, cabinets, or wardrobes, typically with key or combination mechanisms, constructed from durable materials like brass or zinc alloy, and often integrated into furniture designs.

Cylinder locks featuring a rotating cylinder mechanism that aligns internal pins to allow key insertion, commonly used in doors, cabinets, and vehicles, made of metal alloys for durability and security.

Other types of door locks not classified elsewhere, including mortise, rim, or lever locks, typically used in residential or commercial settings, constructed from metal and designed for specific door or frame types.
Various other locks not falling under specific categories, such as hasp locks, latch locks, or push-button locks, used for securing doors, containers, or equipment, often made of metal or plastic.
Clasps and frames integrated with locking mechanisms, typically used in bags, briefcases, or luggage, designed to secure closures with a locking feature, often made of metal or durable plastic.
Parts used in assembling or repairing locks, including springs, pins, tumblers, and key blanks, typically made of metal, used in lock manufacturing or maintenance.
Keys presented separately from locks, including mechanical or electronic keys, designed to operate specific lock mechanisms, often made of brass or steel for durability and precision.
Hinges for doors or other movable panels, typically made of metal, allowing smooth articulation between two surfaces, often used in furniture, doors, or industrial equipment.
Castors for furniture or equipment, featuring a wheel and axle mechanism, allowing mobility, typically made of metal or plastic, designed for smooth rolling on various surfaces.
Mountings, fittings, and accessories designed for motor vehicles, including brackets, fasteners, and connectors, typically made of metal, for securing components or enhancing functionality.
Hardware for doors, excluding glass doors, including hinges, latches, and handles, typically made of metal, designed for securing and operating doors in residential or commercial settings.
Hardware for windows and French doors, including hinges, latches, and handles, typically made of metal, designed for securing and operating windows to ensure weatherproofing and safety.
Other hardware not classified elsewhere, including brackets, handles, and fasteners, typically made of metal, designed for various applications including furniture, doors, or industrial equipment.
Hardware suitable for furniture, including brackets, hinges, and handles, typically made of metal, designed for securing and operating furniture components such as drawers or doors.
Other hardware not classified elsewhere, including brackets, handles, and fasteners, typically made of metal, designed for various applications including furniture, doors, or industrial equipment.
Fixtures for hats, coats, or similar items, including hat racks, pegs, and brackets, typically made of metal or wood, designed for display or storage in retail or residential settings.
Automatic door closers, designed to automatically close doors after use, typically made of metal, used in commercial or public buildings to ensure safety and energy efficiency.
Armoured or reinforced safes, strongboxes, and doors designed for secure storage of valuables, typically made of steel or other reinforced materials, for use in banks, offices, or secure facilities.
Cash or deed boxes and similar secure containers, typically made of metal, designed for storing documents, cash, or valuables, used in banks, offices, or secure facilities.
Office equipment such as filing cabinets, card-index cabinets, paper trays, pen trays, and stamp holders, typically made of base metal, designed for office use excluding furniture under heading 940.
Fittings for loose-leaf binders or files, including rings, clips, or holders, typically made of metal, designed for organizing and securing documents in office or educational settings.
Staples in strips, typically made of metal, designed for binding paper or other materials, commonly used in office or industrial settings for document organization.
Other office equipment including parts or accessories, typically made of metal, designed for office use, such as clips, holders, or fasteners for documents or equipment.
Bells, gongs, and similar sound-producing devices, typically made of metal, designed for signaling or notification in public or commercial settings.
Bells or gongs plated with precious metals such as gold, silver, or platinum, typically used for decorative or ceremonial purposes, designed for aesthetic or functional use.
Other bells or gongs not classified elsewhere, typically made of metal, designed for signaling or decorative purposes, used in public or commercial settings.
Frames for photographs, pictures, or similar items, as well as mirrors, typically made of metal or wood, designed for display or decoration in homes or commercial settings.
Pipes and tubes made of iron or steel, typically used in construction, plumbing, or industrial applications, designed for conveying fluids or structural support.
Pipes and tubes made of other base metals, such as brass or aluminum, typically used in plumbing, heating, or industrial applications, designed for conveying fluids or structural support.
Hooks, eyes, and eyelets, typically made of metal, designed for attaching or securing items such as curtains, garments, or industrial equipment.
Tubular or bifurcated rivets, typically made of metal, designed for fastening materials together, commonly used in construction, furniture, or industrial applications.
Other hardware including parts or accessories, typically made of metal, designed for various applications including construction, furniture, or industrial equipment.
Crown corks used for sealing bottles or containers, typically made of metal, designed for securing beverage containers or other products.
Capsules and bottle caps made of lead or aluminum with a diameter exceeding 21mm, typically used for sealing or securing containers, designed for industrial or commercial use.
Other caps or closures not classified elsewhere, typically made of metal or plastic, designed for sealing or securing containers, used in various industries.
Sign-plates, nameplates, address-plates, and similar plates with numbers, letters, or symbols, typically made of base metal, designed for identification or signage in commercial or public settings.
Coated electrodes of base metal designed for electric arc-welding, typically used in construction or industrial welding applications, designed for durability and conductivity.
Cored wire of base metal designed for electric arc-welding, typically used in construction or industrial welding applications, designed for durability and conductivity.

Coated rods and cored wires made of base metal, designed specifically for soldering, brazing, or flame welding applications. These materials are typically covered with flux or protective coatings to enhance joint integrity and prevent oxidation during high-temperature processes. Commonly used in industrial and repair settings for joining metals like copper, brass, or steel.

Other welding consumables not specified elsewhere, including rods, wires, or fluxes made of base metal or alloys. These may include filler materials for arc welding, flux-cored wires, or specialized consumables for niche applications such as underwater or resistance welding. Typically used in fabrication, repair, or maintenance of metal structures.

Nuclear reactors designed for controlled nuclear fission, typically used in power generation or research. These are complex assemblies including core structures, control systems, and shielding materials. Often constructed from stainless steel and other high-strength alloys, they are engineered for long-term operation under extreme thermal and radiation conditions. May include cooling systems and containment vessels.

Machinery and apparatus for isotopic separation, including centrifuges, diffusion units, and vacuum systems. These devices separate isotopes of elements like uranium or hydrogen using physical or chemical processes. Components may include precision rotors, seals, and vacuum chambers. Used primarily in nuclear fuel production or scientific research.

Non-irradiated fuel elements, typically cylindrical cartridges containing enriched uranium or other fissile materials. These are designed for insertion into nuclear reactors but have not yet been exposed to neutron flux. Made from zirconium alloys or stainless steel, they are sealed and often contain cladding to prevent corrosion and leakage during reactor operation.

Parts for nuclear reactors, including piping, valves, pumps, control rods, and structural components. These are manufactured to withstand high temperatures, radiation, and pressure. Materials often include stainless steel, nickel alloys, and specialized ceramics. Used to maintain reactor integrity, control reaction rates, or facilitate coolant flow.

Watertube boilers with steam production capacity exceeding 45 tonnes per hour. These are large-scale industrial boilers used in power plants or heavy manufacturing. They consist of thousands of tubes through which water circulates to generate steam under high pressure. Constructed from high-grade steel and designed for continuous operation.

Watertube boilers producing steam up to 45 tonnes per hour or less. These are typically smaller industrial or commercial boilers used in factories, hotels, or hospitals. They operate under high pressure but at lower capacity than larger units. Designed for modular installation and efficient heat transfer.

Firetube boilers where hot gases pass through tubes surrounded by water. Commonly used in small to medium-scale applications such as ships, locomotives, or industrial heating. Constructed with steel shells and multiple tubes, they are efficient for low-pressure steam generation and are often manually or mechanically fed.

Other types of boilers not classified elsewhere, including multi-tube, shell-and-tube, or hybrid designs. May include vacuum boilers, flash boilers, or specialized units for niche applications such as food processing or chemical plants. Materials vary but often include steel, copper, or alloyed metals for thermal efficiency.

Superheated water boilers designed to convert saturated steam into superheated steam for high-temperature applications. Used in power generation, industrial processes, or steam turbines. These boilers feature heat exchangers and safety valves to prevent overheating or pressure build-up. Constructed from high-grade steel alloys.

Parts for boilers, including tubes, headers, valves, nozzles, and supports. These components are essential for maintaining boiler integrity, pressure distribution, and heat transfer. Often made from stainless steel, carbon steel, or alloyed materials. May be new or refurbished, and designed for specific boiler models.

Cast iron boilers, typically used for low-pressure steam generation in residential, commercial, or small industrial applications. These boilers are durable, low-cost, and efficient for heating water. Constructed with thick walls and cast iron sections to withstand pressure and corrosion. Often used in older or retrofitted systems.

Other boilers not classified elsewhere, including steel boilers, welded boilers, or boilers with hybrid materials. These may be designed for specific industries such as marine, chemical, or power generation. Often include safety features, pressure gauges, and heat exchangers. May be assembled from prefabricated components.

Cast iron boilers, typically used for low-pressure steam generation in residential, commercial, or small industrial applications. These boilers are durable, low-cost, and efficient for heating water. Constructed with thick walls and cast iron sections to withstand pressure and corrosion. Often used in older or retrofitted systems.

Other boilers not classified elsewhere, including steel boilers, welded boilers, or boilers with hybrid materials. These may be designed for specific industries such as marine, chemical, or power generation. Often include safety features, pressure gauges, and heat exchangers. May be assembled from prefabricated components.

Auxiliary plant equipment for boilers classified under headings 8402 or 8403, including pumps, fans, blowers, and control systems. These are used to support boiler operation, such as circulating water, removing ash, or regulating pressure. Often installed in conjunction with main boilers and designed for specific boiler models.

Condensers for steam or vapor power units, designed to cool and condense steam back into water. These units are critical in power generation and industrial processes. Typically made of copper or stainless steel, they feature cooling fins, tubes, or shell-and-tube designs. May be used in power plants, ships, or chemical processing.

Parts for auxiliary plant equipment, including valves, pipes, fittings, and sensors. These components are essential for maintaining pressure, flow, and temperature control in boiler and condenser systems. Often made from stainless steel, copper, or alloyed materials. May be new or refurbished, and designed for specific boiler models.

Producer gas or water gas generators, including acetylene generators, designed to produce combustible gases from coal, coke, or water. These units may include purification systems to remove impurities such as tar or sulfur. Used in industrial furnaces, chemical plants, or older power systems. Often require periodic maintenance.

Parts for gas generators, including burners, nozzles, filters, and control valves. These components are essential for gas production, purification, and safe operation. Often made from stainless steel, cast iron, or alloyed materials. May be new or refurbished, and designed for specific generator models.

Marine propulsion turbines designed to drive ships and boats using steam or other working fluids. These turbines are typically large, high-efficiency units with multiple blades and rotating shafts. Constructed from high-strength alloys and designed for continuous operation under saltwater and high-pressure conditions.

Turbines with output exceeding 40 megawatts, typically used in large-scale power generation or heavy industrial applications. These turbines feature multiple stages, high-speed rotors, and advanced cooling systems. Constructed from high-grade steel and nickel alloys, designed for continuous operation under extreme conditions.

Turbines with output not exceeding 40 megawatts, typically used in medium-scale power generation or industrial applications. These turbines feature fewer stages than larger units and are often designed for flexibility in operation. Constructed from high-grade steel and alloyed materials for durability and efficiency.

Rotor assemblies and blades for steam turbines, including stator blades and rotating parts. These components are critical for converting steam energy into mechanical energy. Made from high-strength alloys and precision-machined, they are designed to withstand high temperatures, pressures, and rotational speeds.

Other turbine components not classified elsewhere, including bearings, shafts, seals, and support structures. These parts are essential for maintaining turbine performance and safety. Often made from stainless steel, bronze, or specialized alloys. Designed for specific turbine models and applications.

Aircraft engines designed for propulsion of aircraft, including jet engines and turboprops. These engines operate at high altitudes and speeds, using combustion to generate thrust. Constructed from lightweight alloys and composite materials, they are engineered for fuel efficiency, reliability, and performance under extreme conditions.

Internal combustion engines with cylinder capacity not exceeding 325 cm³, typically used in small vehicles, lawn equipment, or portable machinery. These engines are designed for low power output and high fuel efficiency. Constructed from aluminum or cast iron, they often feature carburetors or fuel injection systems.

Internal combustion engines with power not exceeding 30 kW, typically used in small generators, pumps, or auxiliary machinery. These engines are designed for low power applications and are often portable or mounted on trailers. Constructed from cast iron or aluminum, with simple ignition or fuel systems.
